title: Question: Support for modeling metastability on setup/hold time violation
body:
---


Author Name: **Niraj Menon**
Original Redmine Message: 3193 from https://www.veripool.org



---

I'm looking for a way to accurately model setup time violations for a flip flop during simulation.  Currently, I'm using CVC to set up timing parameters for any flip flops in my design.  I use Yosys to synthesize my source code, and write it back out as Verilog, which gives me the structural code with flip flop instances of a predefined flip flop module from Yosys.  I decided to try Verilator to see if I could make it work, but it seemed a bit too complicated to figure out, and I wanted to be sure that such a feature actually existed before I went to all the trouble of figuring it out.

In a nutshell, how do I force the flip flop output in a Verilator simulation to go into a metastable/X state if the setup time constraint for the flip flop has been violated?
